summary: Small decomp-matching PR for common fighter damage code. The diff removes a local helper used only to test stick magnitude, rewrites `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag` to use `GET_FIGHTER` and `VEC2_SQ_LEN`/`SQ` directly, updates `ftCo_Damage_Coll` to use `GET_FIGHTER`, and changes four `.sdata2` symbol names in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`. The bot report supports two new matches: `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag` and `ftCo_Damage_Coll`; the title also mentions `ftCo_Damage_Anim`, but no loaded diff hunk or bot match evidence for that function is present.

key_files.role: Source changes for `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag` and `ftCo_Damage_Coll`; removes `isPointInCircle`, uses `GET_FIGHTER`, and expresses stick magnitude via `VEC2_SQ_LEN(fp->input.lstick) >= SQ(p_ftCommonData->sdi_min_stick_mag)`.

Symbol metadata changes for four `.sdata2` float/double objects around `0x804D84D4`, `0x804D84D8`, `0x804D8500`, and `0x804D8508`, renaming address-style labels to local-style `@217`, `@218`, `@522`, and `@523` entries.

what_changed.text: `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag` now obtains the fighter pointer with `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` instead of `gobj->user_data`.

The local `isPointInCircle(float x, float y, float radius)` helper was removed; its prior call using squared stick components was replaced with an inline boolean using `VEC2_SQ_LEN(fp->input.lstick) >= SQ(p_ftCommonData->sdi_min_stick_mag)`.
`ftCo_Damage_Coll` now obtains the fighter pointer with `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` instead of `gobj->user_data`.

Four `.sdata2` symbol entries in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` were renamed from `lbl_804D84D4`, `lbl_804D84D8`, `lbl_804D8500`, and `lbl_804D8508` to `@217`, `@218`, `@522`, and `@523` while preserving addresses, object sizes, alignments, and data types shown in the diff.
The automated report claims `+804 bytes` matched code and two new 100% matches: `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag` and `ftCo_Damage_Coll` in `main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Damage`.

smart_moves.text: Replaced direct `gobj->user_data` access with the project accessor macro `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` in touched fighter callbacks.
Removed a one-off helper whose name implied geometry but whose implementation only summed already-squared inputs, then expressed the tested condition directly with vector and square macros.
Kept the source edit narrow: only two source hunks in `ftCo_Damage.c` plus nearby `.sdata2` symbol metadata needed for the match evidence shown by the bot.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For fighter callbacks taking a `Fighter_GObj*`, `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` can be the match-friendly authored access form compared with direct `gobj->user_data`, at least in the touched `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag` and `ftCo_Damage_Coll` cases.

When a temporary helper only exists to combine squared vector components and compare against a squared threshold, project macros such as `VEC2_SQ_LEN` and `SQ` may better represent the original source shape and can contribute to matching.

naming_conventions.text: Avoid keeping a semantic helper name like `isPointInCircle` when the recovered logic is simply a local magnitude-threshold condition and the helper is removed by the matching source.
This PR changes certain `.sdata2` entries from address-style `lbl_...` names to compiler/local-style `@...` names, but the slice does not explain the ownership rationale beyond the match context.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Inline the left-stick SDI threshold test as a boolean local before the `if`, using `VEC2_SQ_LEN` and `SQ`, instead of calling a helper with pre-squared scalar arguments.
Use the established fighter accessor macro for `Fighter_GObj*` to `Fighter*` conversion in the matched functions.
Adjust `.sdata2` symbol labels in `symbols.txt` for constants adjacent to the touched damage code; the diff preserves address, size, alignment, and type metadata while changing symbol names.

follow_up_queries: Search later or adjacent PRs for `ftCo_Damage_Anim`, because this PR title mentions it but the loaded diff and bot report only show source evidence for `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag` and `ftCo_Damage_Coll`.
Search `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` replacements in fighter callbacks to determine whether this is a broader local style pattern or only a match tactic for this damage file.
Search `.sdata2` `lbl_804D84D4` `@217` and `lbl_804D8500` `@522` history to understand whether these symbol renames reflect split ownership, local-label metadata, or one-off matching metadata.
Search `VEC2_SQ_LEN(fp->input.lstick)` and `sdi_min_stick_mag` for nearby authored-style analogs in fighter common damage code.

notes: The loaded diff is complete and directly supports the file-level source and symbol changes.
The PR body is empty and there are no review comments or human review notes in the loaded slice.
The bot report supports two new matches and `+804 bytes` matched code, but does not mention `ftCo_Damage_Anim` despite the PR title naming it.
No build log, objdiff details, or commit discussion are loaded beyond the bot summary, so matching tactics should remain scoped to the visible changes.
The reason for the `.sdata2` symbol renames is not explained in the PR text; only the metadata changes themselves are evidenced.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use `GET_FIGHTER` for matched fighter GObj callbacks when local code supports it
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2243, replacing direct `gobj->user_data` reads with `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` in `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag` and `ftCo_Damage_Coll` was part of a patch that the bot reported as matching both functions. Scope this lesson to fighter callbacks and require local evidence before generalizing.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer vector macros over one-off squared-component helpers when matching stick magnitude checks
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2243 removed a local `isPointInCircle` helper and expressed the left-stick SDI threshold check with `VEC2_SQ_LEN(fp->input.lstick) >= SQ(p_ftCommonData->sdi_min_stick_mag)`, contributing to the reported match for `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag`.

source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially useful path-context fact for future searches, but should be curated rather than promoted from this intake.

source_update_candidates.reason: Could inform data/symbol ownership records, but evidence is too thin for an accepted global rule.

rejection_notes.text: Do not record `ftCo_Damage_Anim` as matched from this slice; it appears in the PR title but not in the loaded diff hunk or bot match table.
Do not infer a global rule that `.sdata2` address-style labels should be renamed to `@...`; this PR shows specific symbol changes but no explanatory review or ownership evidence.
Do not infer reviewer intent or requested changes; the loaded slice has no review comments and an empty PR body.
